title: Apparatus and method for packaging tobacco industry products
abstract: The present disclosure relates to apparatus (1) for packaging tobacco industry products. The apparatus includes a path (3) and a plurality of carriers (4) that move along the path. Each carrier is adapted to carry a group of tobacco industry products (13) and a wrapper (12). A sealing station (9) is located along the path (3) and is configured to form a first and a second seal (53,55) in the wrapper. The apparatus is configured such that the group of tobacco industry products and the wrapper are rotated between formation of the first and second seals by the sealing station (9).
claims: Claims Claims 1. Apparatus for packaging tobacco industry products, the apparatus defining a path and comprising a plurality of carriers adapted to move along the path, each carrier being adapted to carry a group of tobacco industry products and a wrapper, the apparatus further comprising: a sealing station located at a position on the path and configured to form a first and a second seal in the wrapper extending about the group of tobacco industry products; wherein the apparatus is configured such that said group of tobacco industry products and wrapper are rotated between formation of the first and second seals. 2. Apparatus of claim l, wherein the sealing station comprises a first sealer to form the first seal in the wrapper, and a second sealer to form the second seal in the wrapper, the second sealer being located downstream of the first sealer. 3. Apparatus of claim 2, wherein the apparatus is configured such that rotation of the tobacco industry products and wrapper occurs as the tobacco industry products and wrapper move from the first sealer towards the second sealer. 4. Apparatus of to claim 2, wherein the apparatus is configured such that rotation of the tobacco industry products and wrapper occurs at the first sealer after formation of the first seal and before the tobacco industry products and wrapper are moved from the first sealer towards the second sealer. 5. Apparatus of claim 2, wherein the apparatus is configured such that rotation of the tobacco industry products and the wrapper occurs at the second sealer after formation of the first seal and after the tobacco industry products and wrapper are moved from the first sealer to the second sealer. 6. The apparatus of any of claims 2 to 5, wherein the first sealer and the second sealer are configured to seal said wrapper about said group of tobacco industry products to form a sealed bundle of tobacco industry products. 7. The apparatus of any of claims 2 to 6, wherein the first sealer comprises a pair of sealer bars, and the second sealer comprises a pair of sealer bars, and wherein the sealer bars of the first sealer are parallel to the sealer bars of the second sealer. 8. The apparatus of claim 7, wherein the second sealer comprises two pairs of sealer bars arranged to form a pair of second seals on opposing sides of said wrapper. 9. The apparatus of claims 7 or claim 8, wherein the sealer bars of the first sealer and the sealer bars of the second sealer are parallel to the path in the position on the path where the first sealer and the second sealer are located. 10. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the apparatus is configured to rotate said group of tobacco industry products and wrapper through 90 degrees such that said first and second seals formed in said wrapper are perpendicular to each other. 11. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the path extends directly between the first sealer and the second sealer. 12. Apparatus according to any of claims 1 to 11, wherein each carrier comprises a pocket to receive said group of tobacco industry products and wrapper, said pocket being configured to rotate so as to rotate the tobacco industry product and the wrapper carried by said carrier. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the pocket is configured so that ends of said wrapper protrude from the pocket before said wrapper is sealed, the sealing station being configured to form said first and second seals in said ends of said wrapper. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein each pocket comprises a pair of spaced walls, and wherein said group of tobacco industry products and wrapper are receivable between the spaced walls. 15. The apparatus of any of claims 12 to 14, wherein the apparatus further comprises at least one track, wherein the path is defined by the at least one track, and wherein each carrier further comprises a track engagement portion configured to engage with the track, the pockets being rotatably mounted to the track engagement portions. 16. The apparatus of any of claims 12 to 15, further comprising a rotation mechanism configured to rotate the pocket. 17. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein each pocket comprises a cam follower and the rotation mechanism comprises a cam arranged such that the cam followers engage the cam to rotate the pocket. 18. The apparatus of any of claims 12 to 17, further comprising a rotator associated with each carrier adapted to move along the path, the rotator being coupled to the pocket of a carrier by a linkage, the linkage being arranged such that altering the distance between the rotator and carrier along the path causes rotation of the pocket. 19. The apparatus of any of claims 1 to 18, further comprising an electromagnetic drive system configured to drive the carriers along the path. 20. The apparatus of any of claims 1 to 19, further comprising a control system configured to control movement of the carriers along the path such that the relative spacing between adjacent carriers is alterable. 21. The apparatus of any of claims 1 to 20, wherein the first sealer and/ or the second sealer is configured to form seals in a plurality of wrappers simultaneously. 22. The apparatus of any of claims 1 to 21, wherein the path is a closed loop. 23. The apparatus of any of claims 1 to 22, wherein the carriers move in only a single direction along the path. 24. A method of packaging tobacco industry products, the method comprising: moving a group of tobacco industry products and a wrapper along a path; forming a first seal in the wrapper extending about the group of tobacco industry products; rotating the group of tobacco industry products and the wrapper; and subsequently forming a second seal in the wrapper. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the rotating comprises rotating the group of tobacco industry products and the wrapper through 90 degrees. 26. The method of claim 24 or claim 25, wherein the first seal is perpendicular to the second seal. 27. The method of any one of claims 24 to 26, wherein the forming the second seal comprises forming a pair of second seals in the wrapper, the pair of second seals being disposed on opposite sides of the group of tobacco industry products. 28. The method of any of claims 24 to 27, comprising moving the tobacco industry products and the wrapper along the path after forming the first seal and before forming the second seal. 29. The method of claim 28, comprising rotating the tobacco industry products and the wrapper as the tobacco industry products and the wrapper and moved along the path.
